ground_truth.title: Why We Built a Database for Relevance (Introducing Shaped 2.0) | Shaped Blog
ground_truth.author: Tullie Murrell
ground_truth.main_content:
When we first launched Shaped three and a half years ago, we had a singular vision: to democratize the personalization infrastructure used by big tech giants like Meta, Amazon, and Netflix. We wanted every company to be empowered to provide users with adaptive, intelligent interfaces, the kind where every interaction informs the very next pixel on the screen, making every digital surface dynamic.

These experiences aren't just 'nice to have' for consumers; they are critical for business survival. At companies like Meta, we saw experiments comparing AI-driven ranking against chronological feeds deliver staggering impact, in some cases up to an 800% increase in engagement. That isn't just a better user experience; it is a product-changing impact.

We call this Shaped 2.0, and we are excited to announce it today. You can think of Shaped 2.0 less like a recommendation tool and more like a Database for Relevance. In the past, building a state-of-the-art system meant stitching together multiple components: Vector Databases for retrieval, Search Engines for keywords, and Feature Stores for real-time user signals. Shaped 2.0 collapses this stack.

Shaped 2.0 is built on three layers: The Query Layer provides serverless, low-latency endpoints powered by ShapedQL, a SQL-like language for composing multi-stage ranking pipelines. The Intelligence Layer defines how data is encoded, indexed, and trained. The Data Layer handles the chaos of messy data stacks.

We believe relevance is moving beyond human scrolling. We are entering the era of Agent Retrieval. While humans will always enjoy browsing, a new user is entering the chat: the AI Agent. In the near future, websites won't just be visual interfaces for humans; they will be databases that Agents query to make decisions for their users.

without: Shaped 2.0 is here: Native SQL ranking & real-time context
